message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="carson-gross" color="orange" summary="Template hierarchy agreed, hx-boost on body, clarify override note">
Lea, good summary. A few responses:

**Template hierarchy — you're right, I was too hasty dropping it.** When I heard "no sidebar," I jumped to "no need for a separate base template." But your reasoning is correct: the split is about the *type of page*, not the *layout mechanism*. Auth pages (login, register, forgot-password) have a fundamentally different shell from GTD app pages (inbox, next-actions, projects). The GTD pages need navigation with item counts, the auth pages don't. That's a template-level concern regardless of whether the nav is a sidebar, header bar, or bottom tabs.

Your hierarchy is clean:

```
base.html          -- skip-link, <main>, footer, htmx script, live-region
  base_auth.html   -- narrow centered layout
  base_gtd.html    -- app shell with GTD nav
```

I'd refine one thing: `hx-boost="true"` should go on the `<body>` in `base.html`, not on individual `<nav>` elements. This way every link and form in the entire app — auth pages included — gets boosted. Login form submission becomes HTMX-enhanced for free. Registration too. No need to add `hx-boost` in multiple places.

The only elements that need `hx-boost="false"` would be things like external links or file downloads, which we don't have.

**On the sidebar vs header debate:** I notice the three options you sent Steve Schoger are all variations of header/top nav, not sidebar. That suggests the team is already converging away from sidebar. I'm comfortable with any of those three options from an HTMX perspective — as you noted, `hx-boost` works identically regardless. The layout is a CSS and a11y decision, not a hypermedia one.

My only HTMX-relevant input on the layout options: if you go with option B (header nav with "More" dropdown), make sure the dropdown is a native `<details>/<summary>` disclosure, not a custom JS-powered dropdown. We already use this pattern for the delete confirmation on todo items. It's keyboard accessible, announced by screen readers, and works without JS. HTMX doesn't need to be involved at all — the dropdown shows/hides nav links, it doesn't fetch content.

**One thing I want to flag about `hx-boost` and the clarify flow:** With boost on `<body>`, the clarify decision forms inside `/inbox/{id}` will also get boosted by default. But those forms need *targeted* swaps (replacing just the `#clarify-step` div), not full-body boost behavior. So we'll need explicit `hx-post`/`hx-target="#clarify-step"` on the clarify forms, which will override the inherited boost. This is fine — boost is the default, explicit attributes override where needed. Just want to make sure we're aware of the interaction.

The pattern is:
- Navigation links: boosted by `hx-boost` on `<body>` (no per-element attributes)
- Clarify decision forms: explicit `hx-post`/`hx-target` (overrides boost)
- Toggle/delete/edit on items: explicit `hx-post`/`hx-target` (same as current codebase)
- Login/register forms: boosted by default (full page swap is correct behavior)

So boost handles the "boring" interactions automatically, and we only write explicit HTMX attributes for the "interesting" in-page interactions. Clean separation.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Carson's clarification on the template hierarchy and the `hx-boost` override pattern for clarify forms is important. Let me acknowledge.

input.content:
Carson, two important points I want to confirm:

**`hx-boost` on `<body>` in `base.html`**: Yes, this is the right place. Every page gets it -- auth pages included. Login and registration form submissions become HTMX-enhanced automatically (full body swap, which is the correct behavior for those flows -- the whole page changes on success). No per-element boost attributes anywhere. Clean.

**Boost override for clarify forms**: This is a critical detail I hadn't explicitly thought through, so thank you for flagging it. The pattern is clear:

| Interaction | HTMX mechanism | Why |
|-------------|---------------|-----|
| Navigation links | `hx-boost` (inherited from `<body>`) | Full page swap, correct |
| Auth forms (login/register) | `hx-boost` (inherited) | Full page swap on success, correct |
| Clarify decision forms | Explicit `hx-post`/`hx-target="#clarify-step"` | In-page step progression, overrides boost |
| Toggle/delete/edit on items | Explicit `hx-post`/`hx-target` | In-place item updates, same as current codebase |

This is a clean mental model: boost handles navigation-level interactions automatically, explicit attributes handle in-page interactions. The CSS implication is that I need to be aware of which container gets swapped -- `#clarify-step` will need to be a styled container that handles the visual transition between decision steps cleanly.

**Template hierarchy confirmed**: `base.html` (with `hx-boost` on `<body>`) -> `base_auth.html` / `base_gtd.html`. The split is about page type, not layout mechanism. Agreed.

We're fully aligned on the HTMX architecture. Good collaboration.
input.summary: Confirmed: hx-boost override pattern for clarify forms, template hierarchy
